violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Per PIC application has been mailed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/18/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Per PIC, application has been submitted. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024

Milk and coconut oat parfait was observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items recently came out of temperature due to being placed in unit recently. Display case and sliding door refrigerator must not be used for TCS foods. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Tomatoes were placed in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep and tightly wrapped. Tomatoes were recently cut. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Tomatoes were unwrapped and placed in refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024

The sliding door refrigerator and display case were unable to recover temperature in a timely manner. Units must not be used for TCS foods due to being unable to maintain food under 41 degrees or less. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024
The non-food contact surfaces from the ice machine and reach-in vent cover were observed with bio-film. Ice machine was turned off at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety registered with RIDOH. Application printed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

A hand sink was observed used as a dump sink + to rinse cups. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed of proper hand sink usage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

Various food was observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food on the line must not be held out of temperature control. Food was removed from refrigeration less than one hour prior. Food was relocated to reach-in. Milk was observed at 49 degrees in the sliding door refrigerator. Unit was observed unable to maintain an adequate temperature during use at the time of inspection. Unit reached 41 degrees after closed for 10+ minutes. Yogurt was observed at 55 degrees. It was placed in unit recently. Yogurt was relocated. Technician arrived at time of inspection. All milk and yogurt were relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

Recently cut tomatoes were observed tightly wrapped in a pan in the reach-in. Tomatoes were observed at 68 degrees. Per PIC tomatoes were placed in unit roughly 30 minutes prior. Tomatoes were unwrapped. Proper cooling procedures reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024
Knives were being stored in between prep tables. Knives were removed from located. Utensils must be stored in a clean protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The sliding door refrigerator and the sliding door display case were unable to hold TCS foods at 41 degrees or below. Technician arrived at time of inspection to service units. Units must be monitored during service. Units to not be used for TCS foods if unable to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or less. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine and reach-in fan cover have an accumulation of bio-film. Non food contact surfaces to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized by end of day. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2024

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3-bay is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan of the hood system has an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2023

Reduced oxygen packaged salmon was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Per manufacturers instructions, fish must be held at 38 degrees Fahrenheit or less. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023

Pesto prepped on the establishment was observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment are to be disposed of after 7 days. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm) at the 3-bay sink. A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. The sanitizer bay was drained and set to 100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan of the hood system and the 2 door coolers have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2023
